cashback.notes: 5% as Fuel Points; capped at 250 Fuel Points/month for the first 6 months, 150 Fuel Points/month thereafter. Fuel Points redemption value varies (roughly ₹0.20-0.96 per point depending on redemption method).
5% as Fuel Points, capped at 100 Fuel Points/month per category

history.oldValue: No additional transaction fee on large fuel purchases beyond the standard 1% surcharge waiver limits
history.newValue: 1% fee (capped at ₹3,000) applies on fuel transactions above ₹15,000 in a statement cycle

source.name: HDFC Bank
source.document: Revised Credit Card Fees and Charges (OPM Pricing)

faqTemplateAnswers.Is this card lifetime free?: No. It has a ₹500 + taxes joining and annual fee, waived on renewal if you spend ₹50,000 or more in the first year.
faqTemplateAnswers.Is it worth it?: Yes, for people who regularly fuel up at IndianOil outlets — the 5% Fuel Points rate plus the 1% fuel surcharge waiver make it one of the cheaper ways to save on petrol/diesel spends, though the low monthly point caps limit savings for heavy fuel spenders.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should get this card?: Car owners and daily commuters who fill up mainly at IndianOil pumps and also want a low-fee card with modest grocery/bill-payment rewards.
faqTemplateAnswers.Does it support UPI?: No. It is issued on the Visa network, and Visa credit cards cannot currently be linked to UPI apps in India.
faqTemplateAnswers.Can I get lounge access with this card?: No, this entry-level fuel card does not offer complimentary airport lounge access.
faqTemplateAnswers.What income is required to qualify?: HDFC typically requires a minimum net monthly income of around ₹12,000 for salaried applicants, or an ITR of ₹6 lakh+ per annum for self-employed applicants.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should avoid this card?: Drivers who mostly fuel at non-IndianOil outlets, or high-fuel-spend users who will quickly exceed the monthly Fuel Points caps.
faqTemplateAnswers.What are the best alternatives?: IndianOil-branded cards from other issuers, or a general cashback card like the HDFC Millennia or Amazon Pay ICICI card if fuel spending isn't your dominant category.
